scenario.prompt: For a major group project, you naturally take on...

options.text: Writing and editing — crafting the narrative and explanations
Visual design — creating the layout, graphics, and presentation
The soundtrack or audio elements that set the tone
Hands-on production — building the physical components

scenario.prompt: Your friend group needs to split a complex bill fairly. You...

options.text: Calculate each person's exact share based on what they ordered
Gauge the vibe of the group and find what everyone agrees on
Think through what feels ethically right to you
Suggest a simple equal split — divide like sharing resources in nature

scenario.prompt: Your school is redesigning the common area. You...

options.text: Immediately start sketching layout ideas and spatial arrangements
Think about what kind of space creates meaningful community
Research and mockup a design using architecture or design software
Write a proposal describing the vision and its rationale

scenario.prompt: You're studying for finals. Your most effective approach is...

options.text: Creating songs, beats, or rhythmic patterns to memorize key facts
Building structured outlines and logical concept maps
Pacing, using flashcards physically, or studying while moving
Forming a study group and talking through the material together

scenario.prompt: Given a completely free weekend, you'd most naturally...

options.text: Train for a sport, go hiking, or work with your hands on a project
Spend extended time in reflection — journaling, meditating, or thinking
Spend the whole time outdoors exploring natural environments
Get absorbed in philosophical reading or deep contemplation

scenario.prompt: Someone in your friend group is clearly struggling. You...

options.text: Reach out directly and have a real conversation about what's going on
Send them a thoughtful message with links to resources that might help
Write them a letter expressing your support
Analyze the situation and try to identify a concrete solution

scenario.prompt: When making a major decision about your future, you...

options.text: Spend significant time alone examining your values, fears, and goals
Create a visual map of the options and their outcomes
Process your feelings through music — writing or listening
Work through it physically — running or building something clears your mind

scenario.prompt: On a field research trip, you're most engaged when...

options.text: Observing, cataloging, and analyzing the natural environment
Collaborating with peers and sharing discoveries
Thinking about humanity's relationship with the natural world
Documenting everything through photography and data collection

scenario.prompt: In a philosophy class debate about free will, you...

options.text: Drive the conversation — these questions feel fundamental to you
Craft a carefully structured argument with precise language
Systematically break down the logical premises of each position
Draw a conceptual diagram to map out the different philosophical positions

scenario.prompt: For your senior capstone project, you choose to...

options.text: Build an interactive app, website, or data visualization
Compose and produce an original music project or podcast
Write a deeply personal reflective essay or memoir
Conduct field research on an environmental topic

likert.statement: Reading and writing feel like natural extensions of how I think.
I can articulate complex ideas in ways that others understand clearly.
I'm drawn to storytelling, poetry, or analytical writing as creative outlets.
I'm sensitive to how word choice shapes meaning and perception.
I find genuine satisfaction in solving complex mathematical or logical problems.
I instinctively look for underlying patterns and systems in everything.
I'm drawn to evidence-based reasoning and skeptical of unsupported claims.
Abstract thinking and conceptual frameworks come naturally to me.
I think in images and spatial relationships as much as in words.
Art, design, architecture, or visual media genuinely captivates me.
I can navigate new spaces quickly and have a strong sense of direction.
I often sketch or visualize ideas before I can articulate them verbally.
Music is a core part of how I process emotions and experiences.
I have a refined ear — I notice subtle details in rhythm, melody, and harmony.
Creating or performing music is one of the most natural forms of expression for me.
The emotional and structural complexity of music is something I deeply appreciate.
I learn and retain information best through physical experience and doing.
Physical pursuits — sports, dance, building, or craftsmanship — are central to who I am.
My body is an important tool for thinking and problem-solving.
I feel most alive and focused when I'm physically engaged in something.
I'm deeply attuned to the emotional states and motivations of people around me.
I'm naturally drawn to leadership, mentorship, or collaborative team dynamics.
People frequently seek me out for perspective, advice, or emotional support.
I find genuine energy and insight in human connection and group dynamics.
Self-knowledge — understanding my values, fears, and motivations — is something I actively pursue.
I'm most productive and creative when I have significant uninterrupted time alone.
I have a strong, clear sense of personal identity and what I stand for.
Inner reflection — through journaling, meditation, or contemplation — is a regular practice.
I have a deep, genuine connection to the natural world and its patterns.
Time in natural environments fundamentally restores and recharges me.
I'm drawn to ecology, biology, environmental science, or conservation.
The environmental crisis feels like a deeply personal and urgent concern to me.
I'm genuinely preoccupied with questions about consciousness, mortality, and meaning.
Philosophy, theology, or ethics aren't just academic — they feel personally essential.
I need my work and life to connect to something larger than individual success.
Questions about the nature of reality, time, and existence genuinely energize me.
I pick up new technologies intuitively and enjoy exploring what they can do.
Creating with digital tools — coding, design, video, or data — is genuinely satisfying.
I think naturally in terms of systems, algorithms, and digital structures.
I'm thoughtful about how technology shapes society, knowledge, and human behavior.
